name: Small loop north of Lourdes
name_original: Petite boucle au nord de Lourdes
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Lourde navigate the foothills of the Pyrenees, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and forested sections. The region features varied terrain, from gentle ascents along waterways to more challenging climbs into higher elevations. Limestone formations and agricultural lands are common sights along the routes, offering a mix of natural landscapes and small village settings. Best touring cycling routes around Lourde The most popular touring cycling route is Small loop north of Lourdes, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route offers a local perspective of the area's varied terrain north of the town. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cliffs of Troubat. – Saint-Béat Old Town loop from Galié, a difficult 31.3 miles (50.4 km) path. This trail features significant elevation changes and views of the old town. Local touring cyclists also love the Lake Saint-Pé-d'Ardet – Col des Ares loop from Saint-Pé-d'Ardet, a 12.7 miles (20.4 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Lourde is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and forested slopes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more difficult climbs. The routes in Lourde are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 110 reviews. More than 1800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lourde's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lourdes?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes around Lourdes, offering a wide range of options for exploring the foothills of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Lourdes?
A: Yes, Lourdes offers 11 easy touring cycling routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41622475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route With View of the Valley – Cliffs of Troubat. loop from Saléchan - Siradan&lt;/a&gt;, which is 23.1 km long with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Lourdes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Lourdes navigate the foothills of the Pyrenees, characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and forested sections. You'll encounter varied terrain, from gentle ascents along waterways to more challenging climbs into higher elevations, often featuring limestone formations and agricultural lands.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Lourdes?
A: Absolutely. Lourdes has 43 difficult touring cycling routes. For a significant challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28934280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cierp-Gaud – Church in Bagnères-de-Luchon loop from Saléchan - Siradan&lt;/a&gt;, an 81.2 km route with over 1500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Lourdes?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Lourdes are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868442480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small loop north of Lourdes&lt;/a&gt; is a 37.2 km circular route that provides a local perspective of the varied terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region around Lourdes offers several interesting sights. You might encounter mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/853251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col des Ares&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1816303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buret Pass&lt;/a&gt;. Other attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gar Peak&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2408018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Just Church of Valcabrère&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Lourdes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Lourdes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 110 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to forested slopes, and the mix of natural landscapes with charming small villages.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, many routes provide scenic views, particularly those that climb into higher elevations or follow river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35611304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cierp-Gaud – Saint-Béat Old Town loop from Saléchan - Siradan&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features significant elevation changes and offers scenic views of the old town and surrounding valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lourdes?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Lourdes is typically from spring to autumn, when the weather is mild and the Pyrenean foothills are most accessible. Summer offers warmer temperatures, while spring and autumn provide cooler rides and vibrant scenery.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through historic towns or villages?
A: Yes, several routes will take you through or near historic settlements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35611304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cierp-Gaud – Saint-Béat Old Town loop from Saléchan - Siradan&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the historic Saint-Béat Old Town, offering a glimpse into the region's heritage.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes in Lourdes are rated difficult, there are 11 easy routes that could be suitable for families, depending on the children's cycling experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41622475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route With View of the Valley – Cliffs of Troubat. loop from Saléchan - Siradan&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, flatter option that might be enjoyable for a family outing.
